reasoning: The Lean statement faithfully encodes Rudin 4.4a. `MetricSpace α/β` matches the metric spaces X and Y; `Continuous f` and `Dense s` match the hypotheses on f and E. Mathlib's `Dense s` is defined as `∀ x, x ∈ closure s`, matching "E is dense in X". The conclusion `f '' Set.univ ⊆ closure (f '' s)` says every point of f(X) = range f lies in the closure of f(E), which is exactly the meaning of "f(E) is dense in f(X)" (since f(E) ⊆ f(X), subspace density is equivalent to f(X) ⊆ closure(f(E))). The informal statement also mentions g, but g does not appear in the conclusion of this part — it is only relevant to later parts of the exercise. Omitting an unused auxiliary continuous map does not alter the mathematical content here.
